title: dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. lillsundica
intro: Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. lillsundica, a rare hybrid orchid, is a plant that results from the cross-pollination of Dactylorhiza fuchsii and Dactylorhiza maculata. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, derived from its high content of iridoid glycosides, including gentiopicroside and gentianine. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of Dactylorhiza × kerneriorum nothosubsp. lillsundica, typically in the form of tinctures or infusions, have been used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems.
